failure_modes.text: Simplification can strip out the technical conditions that the real decision still depends on.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when simplification strips out the technical conditions, edge cases, or math needed for correct decisions in the real domain.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check that simplification has not stripped out the technical conditions the real decision depends on; omitting a critical qualifier makes the explanation dangerous.

failure_modes.text: A smooth explanation can create false confidence when the concept has not been pressure tested in use.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when a smooth explanation is mistaken for mastery even though the concept has not been pressure tested in application.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pressure-test the explanation in use: a smooth restatement that has never been applied creates false confidence about mastery.

failure_modes.text: The model fails when simplicity becomes so incomplete that the explanation is easy to repeat but useless for prediction or action.
failure_modes.mitigation: Ensure the simplified version is still useful for prediction or action; simplicity that cannot be used is just easy to repeat.

premortem_questions.text: Can I explain this in plain English without hiding behind jargon?
premortem_questions.source_quote: write down an explanation of the concept in plain English

premortem_questions.text: What part of this explanation still depends on words I cannot unpack?
What technical condition or edge case would break this simplified story?
premortem_questions.source_quote: technical conditions, edge cases, or math

heuristics.text: Explain the concept plainly enough that any missing understanding becomes visible.
heuristics.source_quote: write down an explanation of the concept in plain English

heuristics.text: Use analogy to connect the new idea to an existing schema only after the core concept is actually understood.
heuristics.source_quote: use analogy to fill blind spots

heuristics.text: Treat simplification as a comprehension test, not as permission to ignore the domain's real constraints.
heuristics.source_quote: simplicity is crucial ... but sometimes the real truth is initially complex

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on explanation-as-test, blind-spot discovery, and anti-false-mastery discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor learning-theory sprawl because the raw source is stronger on explanation, simplification, and analogy as practical checks.
open_questions: The second premortem item is normalized from repeated blind-spot language so the chunk stays reusable across domains.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on first-principles-thinking, cognitive-load-theory, simplification, and metacognition as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on authority-bias, confirmation-bias, and status-quo style repetition, but only current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source has an explicit tension block with authority-bias, which cleanly maps into the current target-id surface.
